title: Perception-action loop driver with budgets and failure handling
description: The orchestrator. Per turn: stabilize → capture core → capture plugin fragments → diff → build prompt → decide → validate (≤3 retries) → act (host or plugin extension) → notify plugins via onActionExecuted → persist. Enforces per-turn (30s) and per-session (50 turns / 15min) budgets. Implements all PRD §17 failure modes: stability budget expired, invalid action retries, malformed model retries, three-failed-turns → harness_error: agent_stuck, plugin exception isolation + auto-disable after 3 failures, runtime app errors don't end session, VM connection lost preserves trajectory, binding-not-initialized exit, budget exhaustion clean end, voluntary done(reason). PRD §10, §17.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Full turn completes against a sample app under all default policies
- [ ] Per-turn and per-session budgets terminate cleanly
- [ ] 3 consecutive failed turns ends with harness_error: agent_stuck
- [ ] Plugin exceptions isolated; 3 consecutive failures auto-disable that plugin only
- [ ] Lost VM connection ends with harness_error: connection_lost; trajectory preserved
- [ ] done(reason) ends and writes final summary

title: Action validator against merged tool list
description: Given a candidate action and the current observation, validate: tool name exists in merged tool list (host core + active plugin tools); args match the tool's JsonSchema; for core tools that target semantic node IDs, the node exists, is enabled, and is visible. On rejection, return a structured failure designed to be model-readable. Driver retries the same observation up to 3 times. PRD §10 step 7, §17.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Returns ok | reject(reason) for any candidate action
- [ ] Rejects unknown tool names, schema mismatches, missing/disabled/off-screen target nodes
- [ ] Plugin-tool args validated against the plugin's declared schema
- [ ] Rejection messages model-readable

title: MLX model provider for Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
description: OpenAI-compatible HTTP client targeting an MLX-served Qwen3.6-35B-A3B at 8-bit. Required behaviors: vision input (image_url base64), tool-call parser per the spike outcome, preserve_thinking=true by default for KV cache reuse and consistency, sampling defaults temperature=1.0 / top_p=0.95 / top_k=20 / presence_penalty=1.5 / repetition_penalty=1.0. CORS-safe for browser-based requests from the DevTools panel. Depends on the spike (D1) recommendation for tool-call parser handling. PRD §16.3, §22.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] OpenAI-compatible client works against mlx-vlm (or chosen fallback per D1)
- [ ] Vision input attached when screenshot is included in observation
- [ ] preserve_thinking=true by default
- [ ] Sampling defaults applied per Qwen recommendations
- [ ] Tool-call parser path matches D1 outcome
- [ ] CORS headers documented; sample server config committed
- [ ] Capability metadata reports vision=true, preserve_thinking=true

title: Model provider abstraction with dynamic constrained-output schema
description: ModelProvider interface with completion + schema-constrained variants. Schema is dynamically composed each turn from the current merged tool list — different plugins yield different valid action shapes. Output shape: {action: {tool, args}, summary_update, rationale?, wait_strategy?}. Outputs that don't match the schema are rejected and re-prompted once. Surfaces model capabilities (vision, preserve_thinking, max context) so the host can default screenshot capture appropriately. PRD §16.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ModelProvider interface compiles and supports schema-constrained calls
- [ ] Dynamic schema composition produces valid JSON Schema for the merged tool list each turn
- [ ] Schema rejection retries once before counting a failed turn
- [ ] Capability metadata (vision, preserve_thinking) surfaced for host defaults

title: Observation assembly & per-turn structural diff (core + plugins)
description: Pull observation bundle via getStableObservation, normalize, compute per-turn structural diff for both core fragment and each plugin fragment. Plugins owning a delta-friendly shape get a structured diff; opaque blobs fall back to a previous-vs-current pair. Diffs are harness-authored and included verbatim in the next prompt. PRD §10, §11.3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Observation pulled in single VM call, returned as typed bundle
- [ ] Core diff: routeChanges, nodesAdded, nodesRemoved, nodesChanged
- [ ] Per-plugin structured diff when shape is delta-friendly
- [ ] Opaque-blob plugins get previous-vs-current fallback
- [ ] First-turn diff treats prior observation as empty without crashing

title: Harness scaffolding, VM service connection, and ExplorationSession API
description: exploration_agent library shell that connects to a running app's VM service URI, performs the binding handshake, and exposes a public ExplorationSession API the two frontends consume. Web-compatible by construction (no dart:io anywhere in this package). Provides typed VmServiceClient abstraction reused by later harness stories. PRD §6.2.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ExplorationSession is the public API and supports start/observe/act/end lifecycle
- [ ] Connects via VM service URI and runs handshake against ExplorationBinding
- [ ] Fails with setup-pointing error if binding is absent
- [ ] Package has no transitive dart:io import (verified in CI)
- [ ] VmServiceClient typed abstraction shared across harness modules

title: Stable observation primitive (getStableObservation)
description: Binding extension that polls framework signals and busyState() from all plugins until the chosen policy terminates, then captures and returns the merged observation: core fragment (semantics, routes-best-effort, errors, stability metadata, optional screenshot) + per-plugin fragments under observation.plugins.<namespace>. Implements three policies: action-relative (default 800ms budget), quiet-frame (default N=2), bounded-stability (default 1500ms budget). Per-plugin observation budget enforcement (default 1KB serialized) with warning + truncation on overrun. Stability metadata reports terminated_by, duration_ms, framework_busy, plugins_busy[]. PRD §9, §10, §11.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] action-relative terminates on route/semantics change OR all plugins idle OR 800ms
- [ ] quiet-frame terminates after N consecutive idle frames including plugin idle
- [ ] bounded-stability terminates on quiet-frame OR 1500ms; honest tagging when budget hit
- [ ] Total observation budget defaults to 6KB serialized (~2K tokens) per PRD §11.4
- [ ] Core fragment budget defaults to 4KB; plugins share 2KB total distributed by per-plugin budgets
- [ ] Per-plugin observation budget defaults to 1KB, overridable via session config; truncation emits a warning
- [ ] Returned stability block includes framework_busy and plugins_busy[]
- [ ] Single VM call returns the full merged observation bundle

title: Core action tools (core namespace)
description: Implement and register the host's 10 core tools under the 'core' namespace: core.tap, core.long_press, core.enter_text, core.scroll, core.scroll_until_visible, core.gesture (discrete kinds), core.system_back, core.wait (rejected if >5s), core.inspect_widget, core.done. All target semantics node IDs except wait/done. PRD §12.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 10 core tools execute against an instrumented app
- [ ] Tools target semantics node IDs, not coordinates
- [ ] enter_text correctly focuses target before typing
- [ ] scroll_until_visible terminates with a clear error if unreachable
- [ ] inspect_widget returns the widget subtree for the target
- [ ] wait rejects values >5s; done emits the requested reason

title: Semantics tree capture with stable IDs
description: Walk the semantics tree, assign session-stable numeric IDs, filter off-screen / fully-obscured / excludeSemantics nodes, and emit role/label/state-flags/available-actions per node. Expose as a host VM service extension. Apps with custom design systems whose controls don't expose semantics natively are addressed at the plugin layer (PRD §6.1: the host explicitly does not own custom-design-system knobs in v0.5; that surface is a plugin concern). PRD §11.1, §12.3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Semantics tree serialized as compact node records (id, role, label, state, actions)
- [ ] Off-screen / excluded / obscured nodes omitted by default
- [ ] Stable IDs persist across observations within a session
- [ ] VM extension returns the captured tree

title: Frame stability signal collection (host)
description: Hook SchedulerBinding to track transient callbacks (animations), persistent callbacks, pending microtasks, frame commit/skip events. Maintain bounded counters and timestamps suitable for stability decisions. Used by getStableObservation. PRD §6.1, §9.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] In-flight transient and persistent callback counts observable
- [ ] Pending microtask presence observable
- [ ] Frame commit/skip events timestamped
- [ ] Snapshot exposed for stability evaluation

title: Plugin contract: types, registry, and PluginContext
description: The load-bearing v0.5 design artifact. Define ExplorationPlugin, ExplorationTool, BusyState, ObservationContext, ExecutedAction, ToolResult, JsonSchema. Implement the plugin registry: registration order preserved, namespace uniqueness enforced (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$), tool names mandatorily prefixed (<namespace>.<tool>), collision rejection at startup. Implement PluginContext with registerErrorHandler, registerExtension (namespaced under ext.flutter.exploration.<namespace>.<suffix>), registerFrameCallback. Lifecycle: initialize() in registration order, dispose() at session end. Per-method exception isolation; auto-disable a plugin after 3 consecutive failures. PRD §7.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plugin contract types compile and have stable API
- [ ] Registry rejects duplicate namespaces and tool-name collisions at startup
- [ ] PluginContext API works: error handlers chain, extensions register under correct namespace, frame callbacks fire
- [ ] Plugin exceptions in any method don't kill the session; logged and isolated per-call
- [ ] 3 consecutive failures auto-disable a plugin with a clear log entry
- [ ] Versioning posture per PRD §7.7: plugin updates that add tools, expand observation fragments, or refine busy-state heuristics are NOT breaking changes; host treats unfamiliar fragment fields as opaque pass-through; new tools auto-appear in the merged tool list
- [ ] Contract-level breaking changes (the ExplorationPlugin interface itself) are explicitly versioned by the host
- [ ] Contract leaves room (in interface and config.yaml shape) for v2 resources/prompts (PRD §8)

title: ExplorationBinding skeleton, lifecycle, and conflict detection
description: Host binding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding. ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized(plugins: [...]) called from main(), kDebugMode/profile gating, hard error if another custom binding (IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding etc.) is already installed, registers the 'ext.flutter.exploration.*' core namespace and a handshake/ping extension. Defines the VM service extension naming convention used by core and namespaced for plugins. PRD §6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ExplorationBinding installs as the WidgetsBinding when initialized in main()
- [ ] Throws a clear error if another binding has already been installed
- [ ] No-op outside debug/profile mode
- [ ] Registers 'ext.flutter.exploration.*' namespace and a handshake extension
- [ ] VM service extension naming convention documented

title: Repo & multi-package scaffolding
description: Lay down the v1 package layout and CI baseline. Packages: exploration_flutter (host binding), exploration_agent (harness library, web-compatible — NO dart:io), exploration_cli (CLI frontend, where dart:io may live), exploration_devtools (DevTools extension, Flutter web app), exploration_router (reference plugin), exploration_riverpod (reference plugin), exploration_dio (reference plugin). Shared lints/analysis_options, baseline CI (analyze + test) across the workspace. Blocks every downstream story.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Seven-package workspace with shared lints
- [ ] All packages compile, pass analyze, and run an empty test suite
- [ ] CI baseline runs analyze + test on PRs
- [ ] exploration_agent has no transitive dart:io dependency (web-compatible)
- [ ] exploration_devtools depends on package:devtools_extensions

title: Spike: verify mlx-vlm supports the qwen3_coder tool-call parser
description: PRD v0.5 §16.3 + §22 risk: the local-inference path depends on mlx-vlm correctly parsing Qwen3.6-35B-A3B's tool-call format. vLLM and SGLang require '--tool-call-parser qwen3_coder'; whether mlx-vlm handles the same parser format is unknown. This is a pre-build spike — its outcome shapes the local provider design and may force a fallback to mlx-lm text-only (losing vision) or moving local inference to SGLang on Linux (losing the M3 Ultra setup).
design:
Approach: stand up mlx-vlm with Qwen3.6-35B-A3B at 8-bit, run a tool-calling smoke test that exercises (a) structured output, (b) the qwen3_coder format with simple tools, (c) preserve_thinking semantics, and (d) image input.

Decide and document:
1. Does mlx-vlm parse qwen3_coder tool calls cleanly? Yes/No/Partial.
2. If No/Partial: which fallback? mlx-lm text-only OR SGLang-on-Linux OR custom parser shim?
3. Required CORS configuration for browser-based requests from the DevTools panel (PRD §22 risk).
4. Sampling defaults confirmed working: temperature=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=20, presence_penalty=1.5, repetition_penalty=1.0.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Smoke test results documented (tool calls, vision, preserve_thinking, structured output)
- [ ] Decision recorded: mlx-vlm primary OR fallback path
- [ ] CORS configuration documented for browser access
- [ ] Sample server config committed for the chosen path

title: Plugin authoring guide
description: v1 documentation deliverable per PRD §19. Covers: the plugin contract with worked examples, the three reference plugins as readable source, conventions (namespace selection, observation budget management, when to report busy, plugin-owned identifier surfacing), anti-patterns (subclassing the binding, hogging frame callbacks, unbounded fragments, swallowing exceptions), and the §20.2 taxonomy (configuration / composition / subclass / reimplementation plugins) for wrapping existing tools. Includes the extension/exploration/config.yaml manifest convention. The guide can be drafted from the contract alone; reference-plugin worked examples are slotted in as those plugins land.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Contract reference complete with worked examples
- [ ] Reference plugins linked as canonical readable source (slotted in as they land)
- [ ] Conventions and anti-patterns documented
- [ ] §20.2 taxonomy explained with examples for each category
- [ ] config.yaml manifest convention specified
- [ ] §7.7 versioning posture documented (plugin updates = non-breaking)
- [ ] A third party can ship a working plugin from the guide alone (no host source needed)
- [ ] Recommends writing a custom-widget plugin for apps with sparse semantics on custom design systems

title: exploration_riverpod reference plugin
description: Reference plugin demonstrating structured state contribution. observe() emits {invalidatable_providers, recent_state_changes}. Tool: riverpod.invalidate_provider(provider_id) to force-refresh state for adversarial/edge-case testing. Constructor takes the app's ProviderContainer. Validates that plugin-owned identifiers (provider IDs) are surfaced through observation. PRD §6.4, §12.2, §21.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plugin registers under namespace 'riverpod'
- [ ] observe() lists invalidatable providers and recent state changes
- [ ] invalidate_provider tool refreshes the targeted provider
- [ ] Provider identifier convention documented and surfaced in the observation fragment

title: exploration_dio reference plugin (validates busy-state mechanism)
description: Reference plugin demonstrating busy-state contribution AND action contribution. observe() emits {in_flight: [...], recent_completed: [...]}. busyState() reports busy with reason='N in-flight requests' and est_ms while requests are pending — this is the mechanism that solves 'wait for in-flight network' for stability policies. Tool: dio.cancel_in_flight() to cancel pending requests for adversarial testing. Validates the busy-state hook end-to-end. PRD §7.4, §18.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plugin registers under namespace 'dio'
- [ ] observe() reports in-flight and recent-completed requests
- [ ] busyState() returns isBusy=true with reason while requests pending
- [ ] action-relative stability correctly delays observation until requests complete
- [ ] cancel_in_flight tool cancels pending requests

title: exploration_router reference plugin (raw Router/Navigator)
description: Reference plugin demonstrating routing observation + action contribution. Targets Flutter's built-in Router and Navigator primitives directly — no third-party routing dependency. observe() emits {current_route_name, stack: [...], arguments}. Tool: router.navigate(route_name, arguments?) for programmatic navigation that bypasses UI hit-testing. Stresses the contract honestly (no clean RouterDelegate API to lean on). PRD §18, §6.4.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plugin registers under namespace 'router'
- [ ] observe() returns routing fragment for both imperative Navigator and declarative Router apps
- [ ] router.navigate tool routes correctly when the destination is registered
- [ ] Plugin works against an app that mixes Navigator pushes and Router config
- [ ] Returns null fragment when not on a managed route

title: DevTools: Timeline panel (live + post-session browsing)
description: Scrollable list of turns. Each row: action taken, one-line diff summary, running summary at that point. Click expands into detail view: full observation (semantics, routes, plugin fragments, screenshot if enabled), reasoning trace, action, validation. Updates live during session; browsable after end. PRD §6.3, §23.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Turn rows render with action, diff one-liner, summary
- [ ] Detail view shows full observation, reasoning, action, validation
- [ ] Live updates while session runs without performance degradation on a 30-min session
- [ ] Post-session browsing of an in-panel trajectory works

title: DevTools: Thinking panel (live streaming)
description: Streams the model's reasoning trace into a scrollable pane as it generates. For Qwen3.6, this is the <think>...</think> content emitted before the action. Surfaces the action ultimately selected and the validation result. Must keep up with generation rate on M3 Ultra hardware (no perceptible token-display lag) per PRD §23. PRD §6.3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Reasoning tokens stream into the panel as the model generates
- [ ] No perceptible lag between token production and display on M3 Ultra
- [ ] Selected action and validation result surfaced after generation
- [ ] Auto-scroll with manual override
- [ ] Provider's streaming API is used end-to-end

title: DevTools: Prompt panel
description: Goal text field, Start button, model selection dropdown, session-budget controls (turns / wall-clock), plugin-enablement toggles populated from the connected app's registered plugins. Hitting Start instantiates an ExplorationSession in-panel. Replaces the CLI's argument parsing for interactive use. PRD §6.3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Goal entry, model selection, budgets, and plugin toggles render
- [ ] Start spins up an in-panel ExplorationSession
- [ ] Plugin toggles reflect registered plugins from the binding handshake
- [ ] Disabled inputs while a session runs; clear stop control

title: DevTools extension scaffold and auto-discovery
description: exploration_devtools as a Flutter web app embedded in DevTools via package:devtools_extensions. Auto-discovery: when the user's app depends on exploration_flutter, DevTools shows an 'Exploration' tab in standalone, VS Code, and Android Studio integrations. Provides theming, VM service connection inheritance, and the empty panel shell. Per PRD §22, harness runs IN-PANEL (no extra IPC) and trajectories persist via Dart Tooling Daemon filesystem APIs. PRD §6.3, §18.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Package builds as a DevTools extension
- [ ] Auto-discovered when user's app depends on exploration_flutter
- [ ] Tab appears in standalone DevTools, VS Code, and Android Studio
- [ ] VM service connection inherited from DevTools
- [ ] DTD-based trajectory persistence path implemented and tested in-panel

title: CLI frontend (exploration_cli) with AGENTS.md template
description: exploration_cli command-line tool. Goal as argument or stdin, --vm-uri, --model, --output, --policy, --plugins (comma-separated). Streams progress to stdout, writes trajectory to disk via dart:io. Right interface for CI runs and batch trajectory collection. Includes a minimal AGENTS.md template under templates/ that --help references. PRD §6.2 (CLI frontend), §18 (v1 deliverable).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI parses required and optional flags with helpful --help
- [ ] Goal accepted as arg or via stdin
- [ ] Per-tier model defaults applied
- [ ] AGENTS.md template shipped under templates/
- [ ] End-to-end: 'exploration_cli --goal X --vm-uri Y --model Z' runs against a sample app and writes a trajectory

title: Trajectory writer (JSONL) with plugin contribution metadata
description: JSONL writer for the trajectory log. Header: session metadata (goal, AGENTS.md hash, build identifier, model identifier, harness version, list of active plugins with package versions). Per turn: full observation (core + plugin fragments), stability metadata, proposed action, validation result, executed action, diff (core + plugin diffs), summary update. Final record: outcome (done | budget_exhausted | harness_error) + final summary. Web-compatible — DevTools panel writes via DTD filesystem APIs, CLI writes via dart:io. PRD §14, §22.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] JSONL has typed header line and per-turn records per PRD §14
- [ ] Plugin contribution metadata recorded so trajectories remain readable later
- [ ] Final record always written, even on harness_error
- [ ] Per-turn flush so a crashed session preserves progress
- [ ] Web-compatible writer abstraction; CLI uses dart:io, DevTools uses DTD

title: Anthropic and OpenAI providers
description: Frontier-API providers for Anthropic (Claude 4.6+ class) and OpenAI (GPT-5 class). Wrap their respective SDKs / HTTP APIs behind ModelProvider. Capability metadata: vision per model, no preserve_thinking equivalent. Apply per-tier defaults: lower temperature, smaller observation budgets, tighter retry budgets vs the Qwen3.6 path. PRD §16.4.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Anthropic provider supports Claude 4.6+ tool-use and vision
- [ ] OpenAI provider supports GPT-5 class tool-use and vision
- [ ] Frontier tier defaults applied (temp/budgets/retries)
- [ ] Both work end-to-end from the harness loop

title: Memory artifacts and prompt construction with dynamic merged tool list
description: Implements the two memory artifacts and assembles the per-turn prompt. Running summary buffer (model-authored, 500-token soft / 1000-token hard cap, rejects oversize updates). Last 3-5 actions buffer with one-line outcomes. Prompt assembler combines AGENTS.md + goal + summary + last N actions + observation + diff. CRUCIALLY: the prompt's tool list is the dynamic merged set (host core + active plugin tools), regenerated each turn so adding/removing plugins changes the action shape. PRD §10 step 5, §13, §16.2.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Running summary enforces soft and hard token budgets
- [ ] Last-N actions buffer trims to a fixed window
- [ ] Prompt assembler produces structured payload with merged tool list
- [ ] AGENTS.md, diff, stability metadata included verbatim
- [ ] Removing a plugin changes the next turn's tool list

title: Runtime error ring buffer with chained handler API
description: Bounded ring buffer (default 50) capturing unhandled exceptions, framework asserts, render errors. Hooks FlutterError.onError and PlatformDispatcher.instance.onError exactly once at host init. Plugins that need their own handlers chain through PluginContext.registerErrorHandler — host calls them in registration order, each returns whether it handled the error. Each entry: message, first 5 stack frames, wall-clock offset. Expose with a 'since' cursor for per-turn delta. PRD §6.1, §7.5, §11.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] FlutterError.onError and PlatformDispatcher onError hooked exactly once
- [ ] Plugin-registered handlers chained in registration order
- [ ] Ring buffer bounded (default 50, configurable)
- [ ] Each entry has message + first 5 stack frames + offset
- [ ] getRecentErrors supports a since cursor for delta

title: Screenshot capture (host)
description: Host emits a PNG screenshot of the app surface. Default ON when configured model is vision-capable, default OFF for text-only models — model capability detected through the provider abstraction; user-overridable. Returned as base64 in the observation when enabled. PRD §11.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Screenshot extension returns a current-frame PNG
- [ ] Encoding/decoding round-trips cleanly
- [ ] Default-enabled flag is wired to model capability metadata
- [ ] Disabled path adds zero cost to the observation

title: Flutter Exploration Agent
description: Build a Dart-based agent harness that drives a running Flutter app via a custom WidgetsBinding, organized as a small policy-free host plus a typed plugin contract. Ships v1 with: host binding (exploration_flutter), harness library (exploration_agent), two frontends (CLI and DevTools extension), three reference plugins (Router, Riverpod, Dio), and a plugin authoring guide. Spec: docs/flutter_exploration_agent_prd_v0.5.md.
design:
## Decomposition (PRD v0.5 → v1 stories)

### Pre-build spike
- lenny-18f (P1, decision): mlx-vlm qwen3_coder tool-call parser — blocks lenny-cx6.15

### Stories (28)

**Phase 0 — Scaffolding**
- lenny-cx6.1 (P1): Repo & multi-package scaffolding (7 packages)

**Phase 1 — Host binding (exploration_flutter)**
- lenny-cx6.2 (P1): ExplorationBinding skeleton, lifecycle, conflict detection, ext namespace
- lenny-cx6.3 (P1): Plugin contract: types, registry, PluginContext (load-bearing v0.5 artifact; non-breaking versioning posture per §7.7)
- lenny-cx6.4 (P1): Frame stability signal collection
- lenny-cx6.5 (P1): Semantics tree capture & stable IDs (host-owned; custom-widget exposure is a plugin concern in v0.5)
- lenny-cx6.6 (P1): Core action tools (10 tools in 'core' namespace)
- lenny-cx6.7 (P2): Screenshot capture
- lenny-cx6.8 (P1): Stable observation primitive (3 policies + plugin busy-state polling + §11.4 budget split: 4KB core / 2KB plugins / 6KB total)
- lenny-cx6.9 (P2): Runtime error ring buffer with chained handler API
- lenny-cx6.10 (P3): Host diagnostic — warn when interactive widgets lack semantics (points users at plugin guide for the fix)

**Phase 2 — Harness library (exploration_agent, web-compatible)**
- lenny-cx6.11 (P1): Harness scaffolding, VM service connection, ExplorationSession API
- lenny-cx6.12 (P1): Observation assembly & per-turn diff (core + plugins)
- lenny-cx6.13 (P2): Memory artifacts and prompt construction with dynamic merged tool list
- lenny-cx6.14 (P1): Model provider abstraction with dynamic constrained-output schema
- lenny-cx6.15 (P1): MLX provider for Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (vision, qwen3_coder, preserve_thinking, sampling defaults) — blocked on spike
- lenny-cx6.16 (P2): Anthropic + OpenAI providers
- lenny-cx6.17 (P1): Action validator against merged tool list
- lenny-cx6.18 (P1): Perception-action loop driver with budgets and failure handling (incl. plugin auto-disable)
- lenny-cx6.19 (P2): Trajectory writer (JSONL) with plugin contribution metadata

**Phase 3 — CLI frontend**
- lenny-cx6.20 (P2): CLI frontend (exploration_cli) + AGENTS.md template

**Phase 4 — DevTools extension (exploration_devtools)**
- lenny-cx6.21 (P2): DevTools extension scaffold + auto-discovery + DTD persistence
- lenny-cx6.22 (P2): Prompt panel
- lenny-cx6.23 (P2): Thinking panel (live streaming)
- lenny-cx6.24 (P2): Timeline panel

**Phase 5 — Reference plugins**
- lenny-cx6.25 (P2): exploration_router (raw Router/Navigator)
- lenny-cx6.26 (P2): exploration_riverpod
- lenny-cx6.27 (P2): exploration_dio (validates busy-state mechanism)

**Phase 6 — Documentation**
- lenny-cx6.35 (P2): Plugin authoring guide — depends only on .3 (contract); reference-plugin worked examples slot in as plugins land

### Follow-ups (deferred / not v1-critical)
- lenny-cx6.29 (P3): Sample non-trivial app for dogfooding
- lenny-cx6.30 (P3, deferred 45d): Empirical 800ms tuning
- lenny-cx6.31 (P3): DevTools mid-turn interrupt (decide cheap vs v2 during panel work)
- lenny-cx6.32 (P3, deferred 60d): Plugin scaffolding generator
- lenny-cx6.33 (P3, deferred 90d): Marionette compatibility plugin (post-v1)
- lenny-cx6.34 (P3, deferred 180d): Plugin contract extension for resources & prompts (v2)

### Build order
L0: .1
L1: .2 (parallel: spike, .29 sample app)
L2: .3, .4, .5, .7  (parallel host modules)
L3: .6 (after .5), .9 (after .3), .10 (after .5), .11 (after .2,.3), .35 (after .3 — plugin guide drafts in parallel)
L4: .8 (after .3,.4,.5,.7,.9), .14 (after .11), .19 (after .11)
L5: .12 (after .8,.11), .15 (after .14, spike), .16 (after .14), .25/.26/.27 (after .3,.11)
L6: .13 (after .11,.12), .17 (after .3,.11,.12)
L7: .18 (after .6,.13,.16,.17,.19) — convergence
L8: .20 (after .18,.19), .21 (after .11,.18,.19)
L9: .22, .23, .24 (after .21; .24 also after .19)

### Open questions captured but not blocking decomposition
- 800ms stability default → empirical task lenny-cx6.30
- Tool-selection accuracy at long context → empirical, validate during integration
- Hard plugin dependencies → default no, revisit if friction
- Plugin-owned identifier convention → captured in plugin guide (.35); convention is 'fragment includes IDs accepted by tools'
- DevTools mid-turn interrupt → lenny-cx6.31, decide during panel work

### v2 / explicitly out-of-v1
- Adversarial exploration (§15.3)
- Trajectory replay under integration_test (§15.4)
- Multi-device parallel sessions
- DevTools panel features beyond the 3 v1 sub-panels
- Trajectory-based fine-tuning
- Custom gestures
- Demonstration recording
- Hot-reload-aware sessions
- Auto-discovery from pubspec.yaml
- Marionette compatibility (lenny-cx6.33)
- Plugin contract extension for resources/prompts (lenny-cx6.34)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] User can install host, configure 1-3 plugins, run a session on a representative app in <15 minutes from install
- [ ] Qwen3.6-35B-A3B at 8-bit completes 'login → settings → change Riverpod-managed setting → logout' on a representative app in <20 turns ≥80% of attempts
- [ ] action-relative stability terminates within budget on ≥95% of post-action turns, including correctly waiting for dio in-flight requests
- [ ] A third party can write a working plugin from the authoring guide alone (no host source needed)
- [ ] Reference plugins compose with each other and with apps using only some of Router/Navigator, Riverpod, Dio
- [ ] 30-min session produces <50MB trajectory reviewable both in CLI viewer and DevTools timeline panel
- [ ] DevTools panel auto-discovers when app depends on exploration_flutter and presents a usable loop within 30 seconds
- [ ] Thinking-trace stream keeps up with model generation rate on M3 Ultra (no perceptible lag)

title: Plugin contract extension: resources & prompts (v2)
description: PRD §8: defer Resources (passive content the agent reads on demand) and Prompts (pre-authored multi-step workflows) to v2. The plugin contract should leave room: extension/exploration/config.yaml accommodates resources:/prompts: keys, ExplorationPlugin can grow getters in a backward-compatible way. Implement when (a) chat UI is on the roadmap or (b) a plugin author needs reference material exceeding the per-turn observation budget. Out of v1 scope.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ExplorationResource and ExplorationPrompt types defined
- [ ] Manifest schema extended
- [ ] Backward-compatible with v1 plugins
- [ ] Reuses MCP/Packaged-AI-Assets-shaped source files where possible

title: Marionette compatibility plugin (post-v1)
description: PRD §20.1: a MarionetteCompatibilityPlugin exposing Marionette's familiar tool set through the host. Three approaches: inheritance (preferred — ExplorationBinding extends MarionetteBinding, contingent on Marionette's class being open to subclassing), reimplementation (bounded work, loses free updates), or coexistence (not viable). Verify Marionette source for subclass-friendliness; if brittle, fall back to reimplementation. Explicitly post-v1 per PRD §18.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Marionette source reviewed for subclass-friendliness
- [ ] Path chosen (inheritance vs reimplementation) and documented
- [ ] Plugin ships exposing the §20.1 tool set

title: Plugin scaffolding generator (exploration_plugin create)
description: PRD §19 mentions a 'template package generator (exploration_plugin create my_plugin)'. Generates a new plugin package with the contract scaffolding, manifest, and minimal observe()/busyState() stubs. Nice-to-have, not v1-blocking — written manually until generator exists. Defer until contract has stabilized.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Generator produces a working plugin skeleton from a single command
- [ ] Generated package compiles and registers cleanly
- [ ] Generator covers config.yaml manifest

title: DevTools panel: mid-turn interrupt support
description: PRD §21 open question: should the DevTools panel allow the user to interrupt the agent mid-turn (cancel a long generation, inject a hint into the running summary)? Default position: yes, both, but only if cheap on top of existing session state. If it adds meaningful complexity, defer to v2. Defaults to v2 unless trivial during S22-S24 implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Decide cheap vs meaningful complexity
- [ ] If cheap: implement cancel + hint injection
- [ ] If meaningful: document as v2 explicitly

title: Empirical tuning of action-relative stability default (800ms)
description: PRD §21 open question: the 800ms default still needs empirical tuning against real apps. Run sweeps against the sample app + 1-2 real apps measuring observation-completeness vs latency tradeoff. Adjust default if warranted. Not v1-critical — can ship with the 800ms guess.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tuning sweep run against ≥3 apps
- [ ] Default value justified or revised
- [ ] Findings documented in PRD addendum

title: Sample non-trivial Flutter app for dogfooding & success criteria
description: PRD §23 success criteria reference 'a representative non-trivial app' (login → settings → change Riverpod-managed setting → logout) and 'a 30-minute exploration session against a real running app'. Without a controlled sample app we cannot evaluate the success metrics. Should use Navigator-managed routes AND Riverpod state AND Dio networking so all three reference plugins are exercised end-to-end. Either build under examples/ or pick an OSS Flutter app.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sample app supports login, settings change, logout
- [ ] Uses Navigator routes, Riverpod state, Dio networking
- [ ] Documented setup steps for ExplorationBinding + reference plugins
- [ ] Used as fixture in S10 / S25-27 validation

title: Host diagnostic: warn when interactive widgets lack semantics
description: Connect-time host diagnostic only. Scan for interactable-looking widgets (GestureDetector, InkWell, custom buttons) that lack semantics annotations and surface a clear warning. The fix in v0.5 is to write a custom-widget plugin (the host no longer owns isInteractiveWidget knobs); the diagnostic message points the user at the plugin authoring guide. P3 because v0.5 deemphasizes host-side custom-widget handling — the plugin path is the canonical answer.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Diagnostic identifies common interactable widgets without semantics annotations
- [ ] Surfaces results to the harness on connect via dedicated extension
- [ ] Diagnostic message points at the plugin authoring guide as the fix
- [ ] Sample-app fixture demonstrates a hit and a clean run

value: When user says 'autonomous discovery' or 'autonomous decomp' or asks for decomposition done on my own: do the bulk of exploration and story decomposition without asking clarifying questions. Read the source PRD/design doc, propose seams, create child stories, wire dependencies, and set priorities autonomously. Leave any unresolved design questions as blocking decision beads (type=decision) or as 'human' flags (bd human <id>) on affected stories — do not stall the decomposition waiting for answers. The user reviews the decomposition at the end, not the questions along the way.
